expansion_prompt: Break this infrastructure provisioning task into these key areas: 1) Namespace creation and CloudNative-PG PostgreSQL cluster deployment with PgBouncer pooler and per-service roles/schemas, 2) Valkey deployment via Redis operator, 3) Kubernetes Secrets creation (DB credentials, R2, Stripe, external APIs, inter-service API keys), 4) ConfigMap sigma1-infra-endpoints assembly, 5) Cloudflare Tunnel deployment and ingress configuration, 6) Signal-CLI sidecar template definition (ConfigMap/Kustomize component), 7) Observability setup: Prometheus ServiceMonitors and Loki log shipping configuration plus NetworkPolicy for PgBouncer access. Subtasks 1-4 are sequential (DB must exist before secrets/configmap), while 5-7 can proceed in parallel once the namespace exists. Each subtask should include its own verification steps from the test strategy.
reasoning: This task provisions the entire foundational infrastructure for the platform: a CloudNative-PG HA cluster with PgBouncer and 6 schemas, Valkey, 5 Kubernetes Secrets, a ConfigMap aggregating endpoints, Cloudflare Tunnel, Signal-CLI sidecar template, Prometheus ServiceMonitors, and NetworkPolicies. It spans multiple Kubernetes resource types across multiple namespaces, involves complex PostgreSQL configuration (roles, schemas, pooler), and is a critical-path dependency for all other tasks.

expansion_prompt: Break this into: 1) Cargo workspace initialization with shared crate (DB pool, health checks, metrics middleware, rate limiting middleware, API key auth, error types), 2) SQLx migrations for catalog schema (categories, products with JSONB specs, availability, bookings tables), 3) CRUD endpoints for categories and products (GET list/detail, POST create, PATCH update) with pagination, search (trigram), and JSONB filtering, 4) Availability checking and checkout endpoints (real-time availability with <500ms target, atomic checkout decrementing availability), 5) Machine-readable equipment API endpoint and OpenAPI spec generation via utoipa, 6) GDPR deletion endpoint and seed data script, 7) Dockerfile multi-stage build and Kubernetes deployment manifests with probes/resources. The shared crate (subtask 1) is a prerequisite for all others and will be reused by Finance and Vetting services. Subtasks 2-6 depend on the shared crate but can partially parallelize. Subtask 7 depends on all others.
reasoning: This is a full-featured REST API service with a shared crate foundation (reused by 2 other services), complex database schema with JSONB and availability logic, multiple endpoints including real-time availability with performance requirements (<500ms), rate limiting, OpenAPI generation, GDPR compliance, multi-stage Docker build, and Kubernetes manifests. The shared crate establishment adds architectural significance.

expansion_prompt: Break this into: 1) Go module initialization, protobuf definitions for all 5 gRPC services (opportunity, project, inventory, crew, delivery) with buf configuration, 2) grpc-gateway REST annotations and code generation for all endpoints, 3) Database layer with pgx/v5 and golang-migrate migrations for all 9 tables in rms schema, 4) Opportunity service implementation with state machine (pending→qualified→approved→converted), lead scoring, and convert-to-project workflow, 5) Inventory and barcode scanning service with conflict detection across overlapping date ranges, 6) Crew scheduling service with calendar conflict detection and Google Calendar API integration, 7) Delivery management service, inter-service auth, health/observability (gRPC health protocol, prometheus, slog), and GDPR endpoint, 8) Dockerfile, Kubernetes deployment manifests, and OpenAPI spec generation via protoc-gen-openapiv2. Subtasks 1-3 are sequential foundations. Subtasks 4-6 can proceed in parallel once the foundation is ready. Subtask 7 has partial dependencies on 4-6. Subtask 8 requires all.
reasoning: This is the most complex business logic service: 5 distinct gRPC services, grpc-gateway REST mapping, 9 database tables, a full quote-to-project state machine, lead scoring algorithm, equipment conflict detection, barcode scanning, crew scheduling with Google Calendar integration, and delivery management. The protobuf/buf toolchain and dual gRPC+REST exposure add tooling complexity.

expansion_prompt: Break this into: 1) SQLx migrations for finance schema (invoices, payments, payroll_entries, currency_rates, tax_rules tables), 2) Invoice CRUD endpoints with tax calculation engine (GST/HST by province, status state machine: draft→sent→viewed→paid→overdue→cancelled), 3) Payment recording endpoints with partial/full payment tracking and invoice status transitions, 4) Stripe integration (create Stripe invoices on send, webhook handler for invoice.paid and payment_intent.succeeded events), 5) Financial reporting endpoints (revenue, aging, cashflow, profitability) with period aggregation queries, 6) Currency rate sync background task (hourly fetch, Valkey cache), payroll endpoints, and automated overdue detection, 7) GDPR anonymization endpoint, OpenAPI spec, Dockerfile, and Kubernetes deployment. Subtask 1 is prerequisite. Subtasks 2-3 are tightly coupled. Subtask 4 depends on 2. Subtasks 5-6 can partially parallelize. Subtask 7 depends on all.
reasoning: Complex financial service with invoice lifecycle management, multi-currency support, Stripe integration (both API calls and webhook processing), tax calculation engine with jurisdiction-specific rules, 4 different financial reports with aggregation queries, background tasks for currency sync and overdue detection, payroll management, and GDPR anonymization (not deletion, for legal compliance). The Stripe webhook handling and currency sync add async complexity.

expansion_prompt: Break this into: 1) SQLx migrations for vetting schema (vetting_results and vetting_requests tables) and REST endpoint scaffolding (POST run, GET result, GET credit), 2) Pipeline stages 1-2: Business verification via OpenCorporates API and online presence checking (LinkedIn/website), with reqwest timeout/retry/circuit-breaker patterns, 3) Pipeline stages 3-4: Google Reviews/Places reputation checking and credit signals trait with pluggable implementation (stub initially), 4) Stage 5: Final scoring algorithm (weighted 100-point system with GREEN/YELLOW/RED thresholds) and async pipeline orchestration (concurrent stage execution, 202 Accepted pattern), 5) GDPR deletion, OpenAPI spec, Dockerfile, and Kubernetes deployment. The circuit breaker pattern and retry logic in subtask 2 establish patterns reused in subtask 3. Subtask 4 depends on 2-3 for stage result types.
reasoning: Multi-stage async pipeline with 5 external API integrations, each requiring timeout handling, retry with exponential backoff, and circuit breaker pattern. The scoring algorithm has weighted calculations with specific boundary conditions. The trait-based credit provider design adds architectural consideration. However, it's somewhat more contained than the finance or RMS services since it's a single pipeline workflow.

expansion_prompt: Break this into: 1) Project initialization (Bun, Elysia, Effect, TypeScript) with database migrations for social schema (uploads, photos, drafts, published_posts) and R2 integration for photo upload/storage, 2) REST endpoints for upload, draft management (list/detail/approve/reject/publish) with Effect Schema validation, 3) AI curation pipeline: OpenAI Vision scoring, photo selection, platform-specific cropping via sharp, AI caption generation per platform, 4) Effect Service pattern for platform clients (Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook, TikTok) with retry logic, 5) NATS integration for async publish pipeline: subscribe to social.publish, publish to platforms, dead-letter handling after 3 failures, 6) Health/metrics endpoints, GDPR deletion (including R2 cleanup), OpenAPI via Elysia Swagger, Dockerfile, and Kubernetes deployment. Subtask 1 is prerequisite. Subtasks 2-3 can partially parallelize. Subtask 4 is needed by subtask 5. Subtask 6 depends on all others.
reasoning: This service combines multiple complex concerns: multipart file upload to R2, AI-powered image curation using OpenAI Vision, image processing with sharp for multiple platform-specific crops, caption generation, 4 different social media platform API integrations as Effect services, NATS async messaging with dead-letter handling, and the Effect 3.x + Elysia stack which is relatively novel. The AI pipeline and multi-platform publishing are particularly complex.

expansion_prompt: Break this into: 1) OpenClaw agent configuration (persona, model, system prompt) and workspace PVC setup, 2) MCP Tool Server configuration for all 10 tools with JSON Schema definitions and REST/gRPC endpoint mappings to backend services, 3) Skills configuration: sales-qual (multi-turn conversation with catalog/availability tools) and customer-vet (vetting integration with GREEN/YELLOW/RED interpretation), 4) Skills configuration: quote-gen, upsell, finance, social-media, rms-*, and admin skills, 5) Signal integration: Signal-CLI sidecar REST API polling/webhook, inbound/outbound message handling, photo support, group vs direct message handling, 6) Voice integration (ElevenLabs + Twilio SIP) and web chat WebSocket endpoint with conversation persistence, 7) Kubernetes deployment in openclaw namespace, service API keys configuration, Signal account rotation strategy, and performance optimization (tool parallelization, <10s response target). Subtask 1-2 are foundational. Subtasks 3-4 depend on 2. Subtasks 5-6 can parallelize. Subtask 7 ties everything together.
reasoning: Morgan is the central orchestration layer connecting all services. It involves: configuring 10 MCP tools with precise API mappings, 8+ skill workflows with multi-turn conversation logic, Signal messaging integration via sidecar, voice pipeline (ElevenLabs + Twilio), WebSocket web chat, conversation state management, and complex deployment with sidecar containers. The integration surface area is the largest of any task, touching all 5 backend services.

expansion_prompt: Break this into: 1) Next.js 15 project initialization with App Router, TailwindCSS 4, shadcn/ui, Effect 3.x integration, TanStack Query setup, and design token system (dark/moody palette), 2) Home page, portfolio page, and SEO infrastructure (Schema.org, OpenGraph, sitemap, robots.txt, llms.txt), 3) Equipment catalog pages: category listing, product grid with filtering/search/pagination, and product detail with image gallery and specs table, 4) Availability calendar component and quote builder multi-step form (product selection with availability check, event details, review & submit), 5) Morgan web chat widget: WebSocket connection, rich message support (product cards, quote summaries), typing indicators, localStorage persistence, minimizable floating UI, 6) Accessibility audit (WCAG 2.1 AA), performance optimization (Lighthouse >90), and Cloudflare Pages deployment configuration. Subtask 1 is prerequisite. Subtasks 2-5 can parallelize once the design system is ready. Subtask 6 depends on all others.
reasoning: Full-featured Next.js 15 application with: Effect 3.x integration (novel pattern for React), multiple page types (SSR catalog, static home/portfolio, dynamic quote builder), real-time availability calendar, multi-step quote form with complex state management, WebSocket chat widget with rich messages, AI-optimization pages (llms.txt), Cloudflare Pages edge deployment, and strict performance/accessibility targets. The Effect + TanStack Query + Next.js App Router combination adds complexity.

expansion_prompt: Break this into: 1) Expo project initialization with TypeScript, Expo Router tabs, design system porting from web (NativeWind/styled components matching dark theme), and shared API client with Effect, 2) Equipment tab: category list, product grid with infinite scroll, product detail with availability, pull-to-refresh, 3) Quote builder tab: step-by-step wizard with native date picker, equipment selector, venue input, review and submit; plus offline queuing capability, 4) Morgan chat tab: full-screen WebSocket chat, push notifications via Expo Notifications, photo sending via expo-image-picker for social pipeline, conversation persistence in AsyncStorage, 5) Barcode scan tab: expo-camera barcode detection, RMS ScanBarcode API integration, equipment details display with check-in/check-out actions, haptic feedback; plus EAS build configuration. Subtask 1 is prerequisite. Subtasks 2-5 can parallelize once the design system and API client are ready.
reasoning: Mobile app with 5 distinct features across tabs: equipment browsing with infinite scroll, multi-step quote builder with offline support, WebSocket chat with push notifications, and barcode scanning with camera integration. The design system porting from web, offline capability, push notification integration, and EAS build configuration add complexity. However, many patterns mirror the web frontend, and Expo abstracts some native complexity.

expansion_prompt: Break this into: 1) HA scaling: verify/update replica counts for all services, pod anti-affinity rules, HPA for equipment-catalog, Valkey sentinel evaluation, and resource tuning, 2) Network policies: default deny in sigma1 namespace, allow rules for all documented communication paths (services→DB, services→NATS, Morgan→services, ingress→services, egress to external APIs), 3) Ingress/TLS and CDN: Cloudflare Tunnel configuration for all public endpoints, CDN caching rules for images vs API, mTLS documentation, 4) RBAC and pod security: per-service ServiceAccounts, PodSecurityStandards restricted profile, image scanning policy, secret rotation documentation/automation, 5) GDPR deletion orchestrator: Rust CLI binary in Cargo workspace that calls all service GDPR endpoints in order, writes audit log, packaged as Docker image with K8s Job template, 6) CI/CD pipeline: GitHub Actions for PR (lint, build, test, security scan) and merge (image push, ArgoCD manifest update), with per-language toolchain configuration, 7) ArgoCD GitOps: Application CRs for each service, sync policies (automated self-heal, prune, rollback on health failure), 8) Monitoring and alerting: Grafana dashboards (platform overview, per-service, PostgreSQL, Morgan), Prometheus AlertManager rules (6+ alert conditions), audit logging with Loki retention policies. Each subtask can proceed relatively independently except subtask 5 depends on the Rust workspace from Task 2 and subtask 7 depends on container images from subtask 6.
reasoning: This is the most complex task overall: it spans the entire platform with 8 distinct concerns (HA, networking, TLS/CDN, RBAC/security, GDPR orchestrator, CI/CD, GitOps, monitoring). It requires deep knowledge of Kubernetes security primitives, network policies for complex multi-namespace communication, building a new Rust CLI tool, configuring GitHub Actions for 4 different language toolchains, ArgoCD GitOps with rollback, and comprehensive Grafana/Prometheus monitoring. Every other task is a dependency, making this the integration point for the entire system.
